texts: I spent most of last year building a distributed sensor network for urban air quality monitoring. The hard part wasn't the hardware — it was getting the calibration to hold up across different weather conditions. I ended up training a gradient-boosted model on the sensor drift patterns and got RMSE down to 2.3 µg/m³ across 47 deployment sites.

open_source.description: Distributed low-cost air quality sensor network with ML calibration

publications.title: Gradient-Boosted Calibration for Low-Cost PM2.5 Sensors
